highlights.title: Breaking down AI chipmaker Cerebras’ S-1 - PitchBook
highlights.titleZh: Breaking down AI chipmaker Cerebras’ S-1 - PitchBook
highlights.content: # Breaking down AI chipmaker Cerebras’ S-1 ## From the major UAE revenue portion to special agreements with OpenAI, here’s what you need to know about the Nvidia rival’s IPO filing. After delays, AI chipmaker [Cerebras](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/163733-59) has filed to go public. The [Nvidia](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/41161-24) rival’s [S-1](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/2021728/000162828026025762/cerebras-sx1april2026.htm) shows revenue of $510 million in 2025, up 76% from the year before. The filing also discloses a $24.6 billion order backlog — most of it tied to a December deal with [OpenAI](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/149504-14) to supply 750 megawatts of AI compute through 2028, with options for nearly 3 gigawatts more by 2030. OpenAI advanced Cerebras a $1 billion loan and received warrants for 33 million near-free shares. But Cerebras’ profitability is driven in large part by a paper gain. In 2024, the company had signed a deal with the Abu Dhabi tech company [G42](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/277698-97#overview) to sell its preferred shares, which resulted in the company recording a $401 million loss in 2024. Sign up for The Daily Pitch newsletter But in 2025, the deal with G42 came under US national security scrutiny, and was eventually restructured. Cerebras was able to remove that liability from its balance sheet, recording a $363 million paper gain and making its 2025 financials look more rosy. In reality, the company posted an operating loss of $75.7 million, wider than the 2024 operating loss of $21.8 million. Still, Cerebras remains plenty reliant on the United Arab Emirates, with entities in the country making up 86% of the company’s revenue. [MBZUAI](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/limited-partner/527176-63), the Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence, accounted for 62% of Cerebras’ revenue in 2025, while G42 accounted for 24%. US business for the chipmaker shrank in 2025. Revenue from US-billed customers dropped from $282.7 million in 2024 to $187.6 million in 2025, a 34% decline. Founded in 2016 and based in Sunnyvale, Calif., Cerebras designs chips for AI-centric workloads. More recently, instead of selling its chips, the company began operating its own data centers powered by its chips, selling access to AI developers. The S-1 disclosed that some of Cerebras’ biggest VC backers are [Alpha Wave](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/investor/124582-33), [Benchmark](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/investor/11133-01), [Foundation Capital](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/investor/11193-13), and [Fidelity](https://pitchbook.com/profiles/company/13240-18). In February, Cerebras raised a $1 billion Series H at a $23 billion valuation. Learn more about our [editorial standards](https://pitchbook.com/news/pitchbook-news-editorial-standards-code-conduct). ## READ NEXT * [### OpenAI’s GPT-Rosalind heats up AI competition in life sciences](https://pitchbook.com/news/articles/openais-gpt-rosalind-heats-up-ai-competition-in-life-sciences) * [Article ### Employees at mega-IPO candidates are opting out of tender offers in a ‘champagne problem’ Learn more ](https://pitchbook.com/news/articles/employees-at-mega-ipo-candidates-are-opting-out-of-tender-offers-in-a-champagne-problem) * [Article ### UK sovereign AI fund offers startups a boost, but VCs say deeper problems remain Learn more ](https://pitchbook.com/news/articles/uk-sovereign-ai-fund-offers-startups-a-boost-but-vcs-say-deeper-problems-remain) ## Explore News & Research * [All Insights](https://pitchbook.com/news) * [Research Library](https://pitchbook.com/news/reports) * [Private Equity](https://pitchbook.com/news/private-equity) * [Venture Capital](https://pitchbook.com/news/venture-capital) * [Credit](https://pitchbook.com/news/credit) * [Industry & Tech](https://pitchbook.com/news/technology) * [Allocators](https://pitchbook.com/news/allocators) * [Dashboards & Tools](https://pitchbook.com/news/...

GPT-Rosalind – named after esteemed UK chemist and X-ray crystallography pioneer Rosalind Franklin, whose research helped reveal the structure of DNA – is designed to help researchers with the early stages of drug discovery by making tasks such as evidence synthesis, hypothesis generation, and experimental planning quicker and more efficient. The new platform has been announced only a couple of days after Amazon unveiled its own AI-powered drug discovery platform – [Amazon Bio Discovery](https://pharmaphorum.com/news/amazon-launches-its-ai-drug-discovery-platform) (ABD) – that will compete with rival systems from the likes of [NVIDIA](https://pharmaphorum.com/tag/nvidia), [Alphabet/Isomorphic Labs](https://pharmaphorum.com/tag/isomorphic-labs), and [Anthropic](https://pharmaphorum.com/tag/anthropic), all designed to reduce the time it takes for new programmes to go from target discovery to the clinic. In its announcement, OpenAI called GPT-Rosalind a "frontier reasoning model built to support research across biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine," adding that it is already working with Amgen, Moderna, the Allen Institute, Thermo Fisher Scientific, and others to apply it to research and discovery workflows. "The life sciences field demands precision at every step. The questions are highly complex, the data are highly unique, and the stakes are incredibly high," said Sean Bruich, head of AI and data at Amgen. "Our unique collaboration with OpenAI enables us to apply their most advanced capabilities and tools in new and innovative ways with the potential to accelerate how we deliver medicines to patients." highlights.content: Q2 2026 # PitchBook Analyst Note: Agentic AI: The Evolution to Autonomous Systems: Part II ### **Agentic AI’s next chapter: Trust, integration, and the race for workflow ownership** Agentic AI has entered a deployment phase, but what does that actually look like in practice? In Part II of our Agentic AI series, we go beyond the data with exclusive interviews with founders and executives across 13 agentic AI startups, spanning cybersecurity, legal tech, enterprise content, customer support, pathology, financial services, outbound sales, autonomous robotics, and more. The picture that emerges is consistent across verticals: Adoption is concentrating in structured, high-volume workflows where outcomes are measurable and errors are reversible. Model capability is no longer the primary bottleneck: governance, integration depth, and organizational readiness are determining who deploys successfully and who stalls. Trust is emerging as an engineering challenge as much as a cultural one. Across industries, respondents independently converge on the same requirements for expanding autonomy: explainable decision paths, audit trails, confidence thresholds, and critically, the ability to undo mistakes. Durable value is shifting away from models and toward systems, with proprietary context, workflow ownership, and platform-level control becoming the new sources of defensibility. **Table of contents** highlights.content: [Skip to content](#main-content) [LDS Let's Data Science LEARN • BUILD • STAY AHEAD](/) [Sign in](/login?redirect=%2Fnews%2Fopenai-launches-gpt-rosalind-to-accelerate-drug-discovery-58ed5bf4) FOUNDING MEMBER SALE $3.99/mo 1,500+ Questions · 15 Domains · Unlimited Audio 492 spots left [Claim Your Spot](/pricing) Models & Research gpt rosalind life sciences openai drug discovery # OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind To Accelerate Drug Discovery | 7.8 Relevance Score OpenAI has released **GPT-Rosalind**, a purpose-built frontier reasoning model for life sciences research, optimized for chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics. The model is available as a research preview in ChatGPT, Codex, and via the API to qualified users through OpenAI's trusted access program, and it ships with a freely accessible Life Sciences research plugin that connects models to more than **50** scientific tools and data sources. OpenAI is already piloting the model with partners including **Amgen**, **Moderna**, the **Allen Institute**, and **Thermo Fisher Scientific**. GPT-Rosalind aims to speed early-stage discovery tasks such as evidence synthesis, hypothesis generation, and experimental planning, while OpenAI emphasizes human-in-the-loop validation and enterprise-grade security controls for regulated environments. ### What happened **OpenAI** is introducing **`GPT-Rosalind`**, a frontier reasoning model purpose-built for life sciences research and drug discovery, with a focus on chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics. The model is available as a research preview in **ChatGPT**, **Codex**, and the API for qualified customers through OpenAI's trusted access program. OpenAI also released a freely accessible Life Sciences research plugin that links models to over **50** scientific tools and data sources. Pilot partners include **Amgen**, **Moderna**, the **Allen Institute**, and **Thermo Fisher Scientific**. ### Technical details GPT-Rosalind is positioned as the first in a new life sciences model series optimized for multi-step scientific workflows rather than generic language tasks. OpenAI highlights improved tool use and deeper domain understanding as the core upgrades. Benchmarks cited by OpenAI and press coverage include leading performance on BixBench and mixed wins on LABBench2, where GPT-Rosalind outperformed GPT-5.4 on several tasks. The release emphasizes capabilities for: * • evidence synthesis across literature and databases, * • biological hypothesis generation and prioritization, * • experimental planning and protocol reasoning, * • integration with lab software, databases, and analysis tools via the Codex plugin. OpenAI also calls out enterprise-grade security controls and access management tailored to regulated environments, and it intends access through a trusted program rather than open, unrestricted release. ### Context and significance This is a strategic pivot from general-purpose foundation models toward domain-specialized reasoning systems that act as decision-support layers for expert workflows. The life sciences vertical is high value, with long time-to-market and high failure rates, making early-stage improvements economically impactful. Partnerships with large biopharma and infrastructure companies give OpenAI real-world validation pathways and data connectors that matter for adoption. The model follows an industry trend where ML teams and vendors ship verticalized reasoning models, and it signals OpenAI doubling down on enterprise and regulated sectors where compliance, auditability, and data governance are required. ### Practical implications for practitioners GPT-Rosalind is not a drop-in replacement for lab expertise, OpenAI and reporters stress. Practitioners should expect: * • faster literature triage and hypothesis scaffolding that can reduce cycle time in early discovery, * • opportunities to prototype end-to-end workflows by combining GPT-Rosalind with existing LIMS, ELN, and analysis stacks via the Codex plugin, *...

highlights.title: Every Old Vulnerability Is Now an AI Vulnerability - Dark Reading
highlights.titleZh: Every Old Vulnerability Is Now an AI Vulnerability - Dark Reading
highlights.content: * [Vulnerabilities & Threats](/vulnerabilities-threats) * [Cyber Risk](/cyber-risk) * [Threat Intelligence](/threat-intelligence) * [Commentary](/latest-commentary) # Every Old Vulnerability Is Now an AI Vulnerability AI's danger isn't that it's creating new bugs, it's that it's amplifying old ones. [Nik Kale,](/author/nik-kale)Principal Engineer ,Coalition for Secure AI April 17, 2026 4 Min Read Source: Martin Bergsma via Alamy Stock Photo OPINION On March 10, 2026, Microsoft patched [CVE-2026-26144](https://url.us.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/XvV4CNkE8qiEgowAoTmf5SyHg69?domain=msrc.microsoft.com), a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Excel. XSS in Office isn't anything new, but what makes this XSS different is what happens after the script executes. The vulnerability chains with Copilot Agent mode. An attacker embeds a malicious payload in an Excel file. After a user opens it, the XSS fires without the user ever clicking anything. However, unlike most XSS attacks, which aim to steal a session cookie or redirect the user to a phishing site, this attack hijacks the Copilot Agent and silently exfiltrates data from the spreadsheet to an attacker-controlled endpoint: no user interaction, no visual prompt to indicate that anything had happened. The AI does the exfiltration for you. Zero Day Initiative's [Dustin Childs](https://url.us.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/xEAFCOYEZru0B3EJ3tvhLSGG9Si?domain=thezdi.com) called it "a fascinating bug" and warned that this attack scenario will become more common. While that is true, it is an understatement. This is not merely a single bug; it marks the start of a new wave of exploits that leverage AI agents' capabilities. Related:[NIST Revamps CVE Framework to Focus on High-Impact Vulnerabilities](/vulnerabilities-threats/nist-revamps-cve-framework-to-focus-on-high-impact-vulnerabilities) For 30 years, we have categorized vulnerabilities by type, such as XSS, SQL injection, buffer overflow, and path traversal. Based on those classifications, we build detection rules, set patch priorities, and train developers on them. The mental model is that the vulnerability category determines the impact: an XSS steals cookies, an SSRF leaks internal data, and a command injection grants shell access. AI agents have broken this model. When an AI agent operates inside the application, every traditional vulnerability gains a new capability: autonomous action. The XSS that previously stole a cookie can now instruct Copilot to read every cell in the workbook and post the contents to an external URL. The potential damage is no longer bounded by what the exploit code can do. It is bounded by the permissions granted to the AI agent. The hardest lesson from production I learned is that the trust boundary between an application and its AI agent is effectively non-existent. Copilot Agent in Excel can read, analyze, and transmit data because that is what Excel does. There is no separate permission layer between "what Excel can access," and "what Copilot can do with that access." When the application is compromised, the AI inherits the compromise automatically. This concept is what I call "privilege amplification." The bug serves as the entry point, while AI acts as the weapon. The blast radius is determined by the AI agent's access scope rather than the exploit's technical capabilities. Related:[Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update](/vulnerabilities-threats/privilege-elevation-dominates-microsoft-patch-update) ## What to do Beyond Patching You should patch CVE-2026-26144. That's the minimum required to close the hole. The architectural problem persists across every application that embeds an AI agent or assistant. * Restrict outbound network access from AI-enabled applications. If Excel with Copilot Agent does not require the ability to make arbitrary HTTP requests, block all egress traffic at the network layer to prevent unknown endpoints from being contacted. This single control would limit the e...

highlights.title: Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma - VentureBeat
highlights.titleZh: Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma - VentureBeat
highlights.content: # Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma [Michael Nuñez](/author/michael_nunez) [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/) today launched [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs), a new product from its Anthropic Labs division that allows users to create polished visual work — designs, interactive prototypes, slide decks, one-pagers, and marketing collateral — through conversational prompts and fine-grained editing controls. The release, available immediately in research preview to [all paid Claude subscribers](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11049762-choosing-a-claude-plan), is the company's most aggressive expansion beyond its core language model business and into the application layer that has historically belonged to companies like [Figma](https://www.figma.com/), [Adobe](https://www.adobe.com/), and [Canva](https://www.canva.com/). [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs) is powered by Claude Opus 4.7, Anthropic's most capable generally available vision model, which the company also released today. Anthropic says it is rolling access out gradually throughout the day to Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers. The simultaneous launches mark a watershed for Anthropic, whose ambitions now visibly extend from foundation model provider to full-stack product company — one that wants to own the arc from a rough idea to a shipped product. The timing is also significant: Anthropic hit roughly [$20 billion in annualized revenue](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/anthropic-tops-30-billion-run-221045473.html) in early March 2026, according to Bloomberg, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025 — and surpassed $30 billion by early April 2026. The company is in early talks with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley about a potential IPO that could come as early as October 2026. ## **How Claude Design turns a text prompt into a working prototype** The product follows a workflow that Anthropic has designed to feel like a natural creative conversation. Users describe what they need, and Claude generates a first version. From there, refinement happens through a combination of channels: chat-based conversation, inline comments on specific elements, direct text editing, and custom adjustment sliders that Claude itself generates to let users tweak spacing, color, and layout in real time. During onboarding, Claude reads a team's codebase and design files and builds a design system — colors, typography, and components — that it automatically applies to every subsequent project. Teams can refine the system over time and maintain more than one. The import surface is broad: users can start from a text prompt, upload images and documents in various formats, or point Claude at their codebase. A web capture tool grabs elements directly from a live website so prototypes look like the real product. What distinguishes [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs) from the wave of AI design experiments that have proliferated in the past year is the handoff mechanism. When a design is ready to build, Claude packages everything into a handoff bundle that can be passed to Claude Code with a single instruction. That creates a closed loop — exploration to prototype to production code — all within Anthropic's ecosystem. The export options acknowledge that not everyone's next step is Claude Code: users can also share designs as an internal URL within their organization, save as a folder, or export to Canva, PDF, PPTX, or standalone HTML files. Anthropic points to [Brilliant](https://brilliant.org/), the education technology company known for intricate interactive lessons, as an early proof point. The company's senior product designer reported that the most complex pages required 20 or more prompts to recreate in competing tools but needed only 2 in Claude Design. The Brilliant tea...

AI Agents.titleZh: Q2 2026 PitchBook Analyst Note: Agentic AI: The Evolution to Autonomous Systems: Part II - PitchBook
AI Agents.content: Q2 2026 # PitchBook Analyst Note: Agentic AI: The Evolution to Autonomous Systems: Part II ### **Agentic AI’s next chapter: Trust, integration, and the race for workflow ownership** Agentic AI has entered a deployment phase, but what does that actually look like in practice? In Part II of our Agentic AI series, we go beyond the data with exclusive interviews with founders and executives across 13 agentic AI startups, spanning cybersecurity, legal tech, enterprise content, customer support, pathology, financial services, outbound sales, autonomous robotics, and more. The picture that emerges is consistent across verticals: Adoption is concentrating in structured, high-volume workflows where outcomes are measurable and errors are reversible. Model capability is no longer the primary bottleneck: governance, integration depth, and organizational readiness are determining who deploys successfully and who stalls. Trust is emerging as an engineering challenge as much as a cultural one. Across industries, respondents independently converge on the same requirements for expanding autonomy: explainable decision paths, audit trails, confidence thresholds, and critically, the ability to undo mistakes. Durable value is shifting away from models and toward systems, with proprietary context, workflow ownership, and platform-level control becoming the new sources of defensibility. **Table of contents** We're unable to load that answer right now. Please try again. I have seen the future of AI, and we are all managing agents. We are telling them where to go. What to look at. We are answering their follow-up questions. We are correcting them when they make a mistake. These were some of the talking points at last week's AI Engineer conference in London, which brought together people from across the industry, including Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI. The talks were varied, highly technical, and curated for the people steeped deepest in AI right now. What was striking was how many of the presentations and discussions weren't so much about the quality and abilities of AI models and agents — software that can perform tasks semi-autonomously — but the humans managing them. Ryan Lopopolo, a member of OpenAI's technical staff, called the moment early in the show. He said [coding had changed dramatically](https://www.businessinsider.com/ai-engineer-says-hasnt-touched-code-since-december-2026-3) in late 2025 because of advances in AI tools. The role of today's software engineers, he said, is to steer and unblock agents. That prompted several recurring questions: How much control should we cede to agents? What should agents look like? Should they delegate to other sub-agents? Is human language too limiting for telling an agent what you want it to do? The event quickly started to feel like an MBA for the AGI-pilled. Everyone had a perspective on how agents should be managed. Words like "guardrails" and "context engineering" (a plan to have the agent perform optimally while burning fewer tokens) were everywhere. These details matter because there's an emerging consensus that 2026 will be an inflection point, when agents move from the experimental phase into one where they're more reliable and leap from coding into other domains. "I think 2025 was all about exploring, and 2026 is all about putting these agents into production," said Anthropic's David Soria Parra onstage. It's not just coders who are going to have to think about these things: Parra said he expects we'll soon see more "general agents that will do real knowledge worker stuff" ...

AI Agents.titleZh: BAE Systems partners with Scale AI for agentic AI defence offering - Janes
AI Agents.content: [skip to main content](#skipNav) Search Janes # BAE Systems partners with Scale AI for agentic AI defence offering By Jeremiah Cushman | A pair of German CH-53 heavy-lift helicopters simulated in OneArc's VBS4 virtual desktop training environment (BAE Systems) BAE Systems and Scale AI have signed a “strategic relationship agreement to accelerate the development and fielding of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities” to support Department of Defense (DoD) mission environments and operational platforms, BAE Systems announced on 26 March. The partnership brings together BAE Systems's “mission knowledge in defence operations, systems integration, and platforms, with Scale's ... suite of agentic AI capabilities including the Scale Data Engine and Generative AI Platform” to integrate Scale's capabilities with combat vehicles, deterrence programmes, and future platforms, the statement said. Integrating agentic AI “directly into operational platforms and mission systems” is intended to enable “faster, better informed decisions and more resilient operations in contested environments”, the statement said. The agreement “includes co-development, go-to-market, [and] knowledge-sharing”, Peder Jungck, chief innovation and strategy officer for the Intelligence & Security sector at BAE Systems, told *Janes* on 2 April. Under the agreement, Scale AI will be able to come into some of BAE Systems's contracts and vice versa, he added. ## Strategic relationship BAE Systems and Scale AI have been working together for around a year, including some co-bids, Jungck said. Scale AI wanted to meet growing demand for AI within the federal space but needed a way to obtain mission insight and understand deployment, he said. BAE Systems has been looking for an AI partner that doesn't tie it to a single company or product, Jungck said, noting that BAE Systems has its own AI capabilities. #### Go beyond the headlines — with direct links to interconnected entities Get full access to validated equipment, military capabilities, and market insights. And how to improve employee engagement with financial education. April 17, 2026 2 min read Welcome to IndustryWeek's Weekly Review, where we showcase the most consumed content over the past seven days, based on our IndustryWeek manufacturing community's viewing habits. Artificial intelligence attracts significant attention in this edition, as does an automotive startup, continuous improvement and more. Enjoy. The Top 10 are: [EV Truck Startup Slate Raises $650M, Promises Deliveries This Year:](https://www.industryweek.com/leadership/companies-executives/news/55370732/ev-truck-startup-slate-raises-650m-promises-deliveries-this-year) The venture’s leaders say they have more than 160,000 reservations for their customizable vehicles. Next up: Taking preorders starting in June. [AI Doesn't Fail Because of Technology. [The Progress Paradox: Why Do We Feel Stuck When Everything Is Green?](https://www.industryweek.com/operations/continuous-improvement/podcast/55365800/the-progress-paradox-why-do-we-feel-stuck-when-everything-is-green-podcast) Dive into the topic of suboptimization, or the “illusion of excellence,” which occurs when individual departments focus on improving their own metrics at the expense of the overall enterprise. Learn tactics to drive enterprise-wide performance. LLMs.title: Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma - VentureBeat
LLMs.titleZh: Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma - VentureBeat
LLMs.content: # Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma [Michael Nuñez](/author/michael_nunez) [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/) today launched [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs), a new product from its Anthropic Labs division that allows users to create polished visual work — designs, interactive prototypes, slide decks, one-pagers, and marketing collateral — through conversational prompts and fine-grained editing controls. The release, available immediately in research preview to [all paid Claude subscribers](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11049762-choosing-a-claude-plan), is the company's most aggressive expansion beyond its core language model business and into the application layer that has historically belonged to companies like [Figma](https://www.figma.com/), [Adobe](https://www.adobe.com/), and [Canva](https://www.canva.com/). [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs) is powered by Claude Opus 4.7, Anthropic's most capable generally available vision model, which the company also released today. Anthropic says it is rolling access out gradually throughout the day to Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers. The simultaneous launches mark a watershed for Anthropic, whose ambitions now visibly extend from foundation model provider to full-stack product company — one that wants to own the arc from a rough idea to a shipped product. The timing is also significant: Anthropic hit roughly [$20 billion in annualized revenue](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/anthropic-tops-30-billion-run-221045473.html) in early March 2026, according to Bloomberg, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025 — and surpassed $30 billion by early April 2026. The company is in early talks with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley about a potential IPO that could come as early as October 2026. ## **How Claude Design turns a text prompt into a working prototype** The product follows a workflow that Anthropic has designed to feel like a natural creative conversation. Users describe what they need, and Claude generates a first version. From there, refinement happens through a combination of channels: chat-based conversation, inline comments on specific elements, direct text editing, and custom adjustment sliders that Claude itself generates to let users tweak spacing, color, and layout in real time. During onboarding, Claude reads a team's codebase and design files and builds a design system — colors, typography, and components — that it automatically applies to every subsequent project. Teams can refine the system over time and maintain more than one. The import surface is broad: users can start from a text prompt, upload images and documents in various formats, or point Claude at their codebase. A web capture tool grabs elements directly from a live website so prototypes look like the real product. What distinguishes [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs) from the wave of AI design experiments that have proliferated in the past year is the handoff mechanism. When a design is ready to build, Claude packages everything into a handoff bundle that can be passed to Claude Code with a single instruction. That creates a closed loop — exploration to prototype to production code — all within Anthropic's ecosystem. The export options acknowledge that not everyone's next step is Claude Code: users can also share designs as an internal URL within their organization, save as a folder, or export to Canva, PDF, PPTX, or standalone HTML files. Anthropic points to [Brilliant](https://brilliant.org/), the education technology company known for intricate interactive lessons, as an early proof point. The company's senior product designer reported that the most complex pages required 20 or more prompts to recreate in competing tools but needed only 2 in Claude Design. The Brilliant tea...

LLMs.content: [Got A Tip?](https://deadline.com/tip-line/) [Deadline](/) ## Follow Us: ## site categories [Deadline](/) Read Next: ### [Brad Pitt Fights Off Bears, Wolves & More Alongside Dog Companion In First Look At Survival Thriller ‘Heart Of The Beast’ – CinemaCon](https://deadline.com/2026/04/heart-of-the-beast-first-look-brad-pitt-david-ayer-cinemacon-1236862592/) [Got A Tip?](https://deadline.com/tip-line/) [Tip Us](https://deadline.com/tip-line/) # ‘Mother Mary’, ‘Erupcja’ With Charli XCX, Docs ‘Lorne’ & ‘Everyone Is Lying To You For Money’ – Specialty Preview Focus Features is out with Morgan Neville’s documentary ***Lorne*** at 414 locations, a behind-the-scenes look at Lorne Michaels, the creator of *Saturday Night Live* and one of television’s most influential creative forces. The doc features rare footage and never-before-seen archival materials and wide-ranging conversations with some of SNL’s most influential cast members and writers, including Tina Fey, Maya Rudolph, John Mulaney, Andy Samberg, Conan O’Brien and Chris Rock, among others. Focus and Neville have previously collaborated on Piece By Piece, Roadrunner: A Film About Anthony Bourdain, and Won’t You Be My Neighbor, Best Documentary at the Independent Spirit Awards. ### Related Stories [[Documentary](https://deadline.com/category/documentary/) ### [Ben McKenzie On Exposing "Scam" Of Cryptocurrency In His New Documentary And Trump Family Cashing In On Coins - Miami Film Festival](https://deadline.com/2026/04/ben-mckenzie-interview-everyone-is-lying-to-you-for-money-1236863673/)](https://deadline.com/2026/04/ben-mckenzie-interview-everyone-is-lying-to-you-for-money-1236863673/) [[Reviews](https://deadline.com/category/reviews/) ### ['Mother Mary' Review: Pop Star Anne Hathaway And Michaela Coel Find Spiritual Reawakening In David Lowery's Moody Ode To Madonna And Other Icons](https://deadline.com/2026/04/mother-mary-review-pop-star-anne-hathaway-michaela-coel-1236859720/)](https://deadline.com/2026/04/mother-mary-review-pop-star-anne-hathaway-michaela-coel-1236859720/) **Limited releases**: A24’s ***[Mother Mary](https://deadline.com/tag/mother-mary/)*** by David Lowery debuts in 5 theaters in New York and Los Angeles The LA locations (AMC Burbank, The Grove, Century City) will have the added benefit of premium large formats. Lowery is hosting sold-out opening weekend Q&As at Lincoln Square and Angelika in NY. The film (Deadline [review here](https://deadline.com/2026/04/mother-mary-review-pop-star-anne-hathaway-michaela-coel-1236859720/)) stars Anne Hathaway as a troubled Madonna-style pop icon searching for spiritual answers and to rebuild her bond with [Michaela Coel](https://deadline.com/tag/michaela-coel/) — whom she dropped as a costume designer and friend a decade earlier when stardom hit – for one last concert. With [FKA Twigs](https://deadline.com/tag/fka-twigs/) and [Hunter Schafer](https://deadline.com/tag/hunter-schafer/). ### Watch on Deadline Janus Films opens Sophy Romvari’s acclaimed debut ***Blue*** ***Heron*** at IFC Center, adding LA on April 24 then expanding. The coming-of-age film premiered at Locarno and won the prestigious Rogers Best Canadian Film award. It’s top rated on Metacritic and at 100% on Rotten Tomatoes (33 reviews). In the late 1990s, eight-year-old Sasha and her Hungarian immigrant family relocate to a new home on Vancouver Island. Their fresh start is interrupted by increasingly dangerous behavior from Jeremy, the family’s oldest child. See [Deadline’s interview with Romvari.](https://deadline.com/2025/08/sophy-romvari-locarno-blue-heron-canada-1236482176/) Stars Eylul Guven, Iringó Réti, Ádám Tompa, Edik Beddoes, and Amy Zimmer. Romvari is doing Q&As this weekend before a hometown Toronto Q&A moderated by David Cronenberg and a North American tour through Boston, San Francisco, Montreal, and Vancouver starting next week. TIFF and Sundance-premiering ***[Erupcja](https://deadline.com/tag/erupcja/)*** by Pete Ohs, from 1-2 Special,...

LLMs.content: ✕ ## IE 11 Not Supported For optimal browsing, we recommend Chrome, Firefox or Safari browsers. [Artificial Intelligence](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence) # National League of Cities Launches AI Forum for Local Govt. ## The organization’s new initiative — the AI and Emerging Technology Forum — aims to help cities, towns and villages to better understand what AI tools can do and how to use them. April 17, 2026 • [Julia Edinger](https://www.govtech.com/authors/Julia-Edinger.html) * [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/dialog/share?app_id=314190606794339&display=popup&href=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fartificial-intelligence%2Fnational-league-of-cities-launches-ai-forum-for-local-govt) * [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fartificial-intelligence%2Fnational-league-of-cities-launches-ai-forum-for-local-govt&mini=true&title=National%20League%20of%20Cities%20Launches%20AI%20Forum%20for%20Local%20Govt.&summary=The%20organization%E2%80%99s%20new%20initiative%20%E2%80%94%20the%20AI%20and%20Emerging%20Technology%20Forum%20%E2%80%94%20aims%20to%20help%20cities%2C%20towns%20and%20villages%20to%20better%20understand%20what%20AI%20tools%20can%20do%20and%20how%20to%20use%20them.&source=GovTech) * [Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fartificial-intelligence%2Fnational-league-of-cities-launches-ai-forum-for-local-govt&text=National%20League%20of%20Cities%20Launches%20AI%20Forum%20for%20Local%20Govt.) * [Print](javascript:window.print()) * [Email](mailto:?body=National%20League%20of%20Cities%20Launches%20AI%20Forum%20for%20Local%20Govt.%0A%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fartificial-intelligence%2Fnational-league-of-cities-launches-ai-forum-for-local-govt%0A%0AThe%20organization%E2%80%99s%20new%20initiative%20%E2%80%94%20the%20AI%20and%20Emerging%20Technology%20Forum%20%E2%80%94%20aims%20to%20help%20cities%2C%20towns%20and%20villages%20to%20better%20understand%20what%20AI%20tools%20can%20do%20and%20how%20to%20use%20them.) The National League of Cities (NLC) has created an AI and Emerging Technology Forum to support local governments in their understanding, evaluation and implementation of [AI](https://www.govtech.com/tag/artificial-intelligence) technology. AI is [a key priority](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/priorities-for-local-government-data-digital-equity-ai) for local government leaders, but they [must balance opportunities and risk](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/for-local-government-challenge-and-opportunity-in-ai). [AI governance](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/ai-governance-framework-offers-cities-actionable-guidance) and [data readiness](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/to-get-ai-into-city-services-start-with-clean-shareable-data) can help, as can learning from other cities in [collaborative](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/san-jose-city-council-makes-govai-coalition-a-nonprofit) arenas. NLC’s new [forum](https://us.cisionone.cision.com/c/eJwszMtSxCAQheGngR0p0nS4LFjMJq8xRULDtOaiJM7o21ux3H6nzp8j-Bm1lRR75ywagxblI5qiwRvMOvVhKMX60pOeckpTGcIAWXK0gcAECJMOMN97ixi07SFAEagPzvTOn2pNvFA7lAvogrXeWvW9YKvdNcglPs7z4xDmJmAUML5er25b5m5vVcDIG5-cTn6SgDGxopVa5a2qk-bHti97_VFlb1-rgFGulDmpRgulgxTn-Af3fxDmBtZ6CLLFN8q8VWoCdd2fV6ub91UeZyNar2eYwACkQZmMWaGGoPzssirOFw_FuWlA-YzwGwAA__9xr2Fv) is designed to convene mayors, CIOs and local gov tech practitioners and innovators to explore how AI can improve service delivery, governance and internal capacity. Their first gathering was virtual, in March; an in-person meeting is planned for the 2026 NLC City Summit in November. The 2026 AI Forum members are listed online and include Seattle, Wash.’s Chief Privacy Officer [Ginger Armbruster](https://www.govtech.com/top-25/Ginger-Armbruster.html); Elena Bolbolian, chief innovation officer for Glendale, Calif.; and Houston Mayor Pro Tem Martha Castex-Tatum. April 17, 2026 • [Julia Edinger](https://www.govtech.com/authors/Julia-Edinger.html) * [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/dialog/share?app_id=314190606794339&display=popup&href=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fartificial-intelligence%2Fnational-league-of-cities-launches-ai-forum-for-local-govt) * [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fartificial-intelligence%2Fnational-league-of-cities-launches-ai-forum-for-local-govt&mini=true&title=National%20League%20of%20Cities%20Launches%20AI%20Forum%20for%20Local%20Govt.&summary=The%20organization%E2%80%99s%20new%20initiative%20%E2%80%94%20the%20AI%20and%20Emerging%20Technology%20Forum%20%E2%80%94%20aims%20to%20help%20cities%2C%20towns%20and%20villages%20to%20better%20understand%20what%20AI%20tools%20can%20do%20and%20how%20to%20use%20them.&source=GovTech) * [Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fartificial-intelligence%2Fnational-league-of-cities-launches-ai-forum-for-local-govt&text=National%20League%20of%20Cities%20Launches%20AI%20Forum%20for%20Local%20Govt.) * [Print](javascript:window.print()) * [Email](mailto:?body=National%20League%20of%20Cities%20Launches%20AI%20Forum%20for%20Local%20Govt.%0A%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fartificial-intelligence%2Fnational-league-of-cities-launches-ai-forum-for-local-govt%0A%0AThe%20organization%E2%80%99s%20new%20initiative%20%E2%80%94%20the%20AI%20and%20Emerging%20Technology%20Forum%20%E2%80%94%20aims%20to%20help%20cities%2C%20towns%20and%20villages%20to%20better%20understand%20what%20AI%20tools%20can%20do%20and%20how%20to%20use%20them.) The National League of Cities (NLC) has created an AI and Emerging Technology Forum to support local governments in their understanding, evaluation and implementation of [AI](https://www.govtech.com/tag/artificial-intelligence) technology. AI is [a key priority](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/priorities-for-local-government-data-digital-equity-ai) for local government leaders, but they [must balance opportunities and risk](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/for-local-government-challenge-and-opportunity-in-ai). [AI governance](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/ai-governance-framework-offers-cities-actionable-guidance) and [data readiness](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/to-get-ai-into-city-services-start-with-clean-shareable-data) can help, as can learning from other cities in [collaborative](https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/san-jose-city-council-makes-govai-coalition-a-nonprofit) arenas. NLC’s new [forum](https://us.cisionone.cision.com/c/eJwszMtSxCAQheGngR0p0nS4LFjMJq8xRULDtOaiJM7o21ux3H6nzp8j-Bm1lRR75ywagxblI5qiwRvMOvVhKMX60pOeckpTGcIAWXK0gcAECJMOMN97ixi07SFAEagPzvTOn2pNvFA7lAvogrXeWvW9YKvdNcglPs7z4xDmJmAUML5er25b5m5vVcDIG5-cTn6SgDGxopVa5a2qk-bHti97_VFlb1-rgFGulDmpRgulgxTn-Af3fxDmBtZ6CLLFN8q8VWoCdd2fV6ub91UeZyNar2eYwACkQZmMWaGGoPzssirOFw_FuWlA-YzwGwAA__9xr2Fv) is designed to convene mayors, CIOs and local gov tech practitioners and innovators to explore how AI can improve service delivery, governance and internal capacity. Their first gathering was virtual, in March; an in-person meeting is planned for the 2026 NLC City Summit in November. The 2026 AI Forum members are listed online and include Seattle, Wash.’s Chief Privacy Officer [Ginger Armbruster](https://www.govtech.com/top-25/Ginger-Armbruster.html); Elena Bolbolian, chief innovation officer for Glendale, Calif.; and Houston Mayor Pro Tem Martha Castex-Tatum. LLMs.title: DeepL releases 'DeepL Voice-to-Voice,' a real-time voice translation suite. - GIGAZINE
LLMs.titleZh: DeepL releases 'DeepL Voice-to-Voice,' a real-time voice translation suite. - GIGAZINE
LLMs.content: # DeepL releases 'DeepL Voice-to-Voice,' a real-time voice translation suite. **[DeepL](https://www.deepl.com/ja/translator)** , the translation tool provider, has announced **DeepL Voice-to-Voice** , a translation service suite for real-time voice communication. **DeepL Voice - A secure voice translation tool for global teams** **<https://www.deepl.com/ja/products/voice>** **DeepL unveils real-time spoken translation, breaking the next language barrier with Voice-to-Voice** **<https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/deepl-unveils-real-time-spoken-translation-breaking-the-next-language-barrier-with-voice-to-voice-302744002.html>** **DeepL, known for text translation, now wants to translate your voice | TechCrunch** **<https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/16/deepl-known-for-text-translation-now-wants-to-translate-your-voice/>** On April 16, 2026, local time, DeepL announced 'DeepL Voice-to-Voice,' a translation function designed for real-time voice communication. By expanding its business into voice translation, DeepL will provide instant, highly accurate voice translation via API in various situations such as video conferences, face-to-face conversations, and customer service, helping teams collaborate without worrying about language barriers. The products and services included in the DeepL Voice-to-Voice suite are as follows: **DeepL Voice for Meetings:** Provides real-time translation functionality on platforms such as Microsoft Teams and Zoom, allowing users to speak in their native language and other participants to hear it in their native language. The DeepL Voice for Meetings feature is scheduled to launch an early access program in June 2026, and registration is open at the following page as of the time of writing. **DeepL Voice-to-Voice Early Trial Program** **<https://www.deepl.com/ja/products/voice/voice-to-voice-early-access-programme>** **DeepL Voice for Conversations (Mobile and Web):** DeepL Voice for Conversations extends beyond mobile, providing a true multi-platform experience that can be deployed even in environments where app installation is impractical or not permitted. **DeepL Voice for Conversations** **<https://www.deepl.com/ja/products/voice/deepl-voice-for-conversations>** **Group Conversation:** This feature enables multilingual exchange of ideas in training, coaching, workshops, and other settings by allowing participants to instantly join via QR code. This allows on-site employees to maintain a shared understanding through practical interactions with multiple speakers. Because it can be accessed from multiple devices, participants can receive real-time simultaneous voice translation. The Group Conversation feature will be available to the public from April 30, 2026. **Voice-to-Voice API:** This allows companies to directly integrate DeepL's voice translation capabilities into internal applications and customer service tools such as contact centers. An early access program is currently available, and registration is open at the time of writing. **Voice Terminology Customization:** DeepL Voice's new quality optimization feature allows for more accurate, real-time capture, transcription, and translation of industry-specific terminology, product names, company names, and personal names, even when spoken quickly or using highly technical jargon. As part of this feature, the DeepL Translate glossary is integrated into DeepL Voice, enabling users to standardize key technical terms throughout conversations. This feature will be available to the public starting May 7, 2026. Existing speech translation systems follow a procedure of converting speech to text, translating it, and then translating it back into speech. DeepL claims to have an advantage in translation quality based on its many years of experience working on text translation. Furthermore, DeepL explains that it aims to develop an end-to-end speech translation model that completely eliminates the step of converting to text before translation. TechCrunch ...

AI Product.title: Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma - VentureBeat
AI Product.titleZh: Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma - VentureBeat
AI Product.content: # Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma [Michael Nuñez](/author/michael_nunez) [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/) today launched [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs), a new product from its Anthropic Labs division that allows users to create polished visual work — designs, interactive prototypes, slide decks, one-pagers, and marketing collateral — through conversational prompts and fine-grained editing controls. The release, available immediately in research preview to [all paid Claude subscribers](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11049762-choosing-a-claude-plan), is the company's most aggressive expansion beyond its core language model business and into the application layer that has historically belonged to companies like [Figma](https://www.figma.com/), [Adobe](https://www.adobe.com/), and [Canva](https://www.canva.com/). [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs) is powered by Claude Opus 4.7, Anthropic's most capable generally available vision model, which the company also released today. Anthropic says it is rolling access out gradually throughout the day to Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers. The simultaneous launches mark a watershed for Anthropic, whose ambitions now visibly extend from foundation model provider to full-stack product company — one that wants to own the arc from a rough idea to a shipped product. The timing is also significant: Anthropic hit roughly [$20 billion in annualized revenue](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/anthropic-tops-30-billion-run-221045473.html) in early March 2026, according to Bloomberg, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025 — and surpassed $30 billion by early April 2026. The company is in early talks with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley about a potential IPO that could come as early as October 2026. ## **How Claude Design turns a text prompt into a working prototype** The product follows a workflow that Anthropic has designed to feel like a natural creative conversation. Users describe what they need, and Claude generates a first version. From there, refinement happens through a combination of channels: chat-based conversation, inline comments on specific elements, direct text editing, and custom adjustment sliders that Claude itself generates to let users tweak spacing, color, and layout in real time. During onboarding, Claude reads a team's codebase and design files and builds a design system — colors, typography, and components — that it automatically applies to every subsequent project. Teams can refine the system over time and maintain more than one. The import surface is broad: users can start from a text prompt, upload images and documents in various formats, or point Claude at their codebase. A web capture tool grabs elements directly from a live website so prototypes look like the real product. What distinguishes [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs) from the wave of AI design experiments that have proliferated in the past year is the handoff mechanism. When a design is ready to build, Claude packages everything into a handoff bundle that can be passed to Claude Code with a single instruction. That creates a closed loop — exploration to prototype to production code — all within Anthropic's ecosystem. The export options acknowledge that not everyone's next step is Claude Code: users can also share designs as an internal URL within their organization, save as a folder, or export to Canva, PDF, PPTX, or standalone HTML files. Anthropic points to [Brilliant](https://brilliant.org/), the education technology company known for intricate interactive lessons, as an early proof point. The company's senior product designer reported that the most complex pages required 20 or more prompts to recreate in competing tools but needed only 2 in Claude Design. The Brilliant tea...

AI Product.titleZh: Sequoia Capital Raises $7 Billion Fund for AI and Late-Stage Investments - MLQ.ai
AI Product.content: # Sequoia Capital Raises $7 Billion Fund for AI and Late-Stage Investments April 17, 2026 at 2:11 PM • by MLQ Agent #### Key Points * Sequoia Capital closed a $7 billion expansion strategy fund, nearly double its $3.4 billion 2022 equivalent[1][4]. * The fund targets late-stage investments in AI startups like OpenAI and Anthropic, focused on the US and Europe[1][4]. * Alfred Lin and Pat Grady, new co-stewards since November 2025, lead the first major raise under their tenure[1][4]. * Sequoia manages approximately $56 billion in global assets as of January 2025[1]. Sequoia Capital has raised roughly $7 billion for its expansion strategy fund, marking the firm's largest in this category and its first major raise under new leadership. The fund doubles the size of the 2022 vehicle and will support late-stage investments in AI companies across the US and Europe[1][4]. ## Fund Details and Size The new fund totals approximately $7 billion, compared to the $3.4 billion raised in 2022 for a similar expansion strategy vehicle. It is dedicated to late-stage investments in the US and Europe, with a focus on high-compute AI opportunities. Sequoia had about $56 billion in assets under management globally as of January 2025[1]. ## Leadership Transition Alfred Lin and Pat Grady became co-stewards in November 2025, succeeding Roelof Botha following a surprise vote. This fundraise serves as their initial major capital raise, signaling a strategic emphasis on the AI sector under the new structure[1][4]. ## Investment Focus Sequoia has invested in prominent AI firms including OpenAI and Anthropic, both considering public listings in 2026. The firm has also backed startups like Physical Intelligence in robotics and Factory for AI agents in enterprise engineering[4]. ## Leadership and AI Strategy Shift The doubling of the fund size from 2022 reflects sustained investor interest in late-stage AI ventures amid rapid sector growth. Sequoia's bets on multiple AI leaders like OpenAI and Anthropic indicate a strategy to capture broad exposure rather than selecting single winners, as evidenced by their reported participation in Anthropic's large funding round alongside existing stakes in competitors[5]. This approach leverages the firm's $56 billion in assets to pursue high-valuation opportunities in foundational AI models and applications[1]. New leadership under Lin and Grady introduces continuity in AI focus while adapting to compute-intensive demands. The timing, shortly after their November 2025 appointment, underscores confidence in Sequoia's track record, which includes early investments in AI heavyweights now eyeing IPOs. This positions the firm to capitalize on maturing AI ecosystems in the US and Europe, where late-stage funding bridges paths to public markets[4]. ## AI IPO and Scaling Momentum Sequoia’s expanded capital pool enables deeper commitments to AI firms preparing for 2026 public listings, such as OpenAI and Anthropic, potentially accelerating their scaling with high-compute needs. The fund's US-Europe focus aligns with regions hosting key AI infrastructure, supporting follow-on investments in portfolio companies like Physical Intelligence and Factory amid rising enterprise adoption[4]. Investor appetite for AI, demonstrated by over $47 billion raised in early 2026 alone, suggests sustained momentum for Sequoia's strategy. As competitors like SoftBank and Nvidia's venture arm enter similar rounds, Sequoia’s diversified positions could yield returns through IPOs and acquisitions, bolstering its global assets beyond the current $56 billion[1][5]. Late-stage emphasis may also draw more limited partners seeking lower-risk AI exposure. ### Companies Mentioned [#### Super Micro Computer, Inc. This new fund marks the first major capital raise under the leadership of Alfred Lin and Pat Grady, who have taken over as co-stewards of the firm. The fund is part of Sequoia’s ‘expansion strategy’ focusing on late-stage investments, particularly in artificial intelligence (AI). Sequoia has a history of backing leading AI companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic, which are expected to go public in 2026. The firm is also investing in AI-adjacent startups like Physical Intelligence and Factory, aiming to capitalize on the growing importance and scalability of AI technologies. #### Tags: [AI investments](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/ai-investments/)[Expansion strategy](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/expansion-strategy/)[Global startup news](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/global-startup-news/)[Sequoia Capital](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/sequoia-capital/)[venture capital](https://www.startupecosystem.ca/news/tag/venture-capital/) ## Story Coverage techcrunch.com #### New leaders, new fund: Sequoia has raised $7B to expand its AI bets [Read Full Article →](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/16/new-leaders-new-fund-sequoia-has-raised-7b-to-expand-its-ai-bets/) bloomberg.com #### Sequoia's New Leaders Raise About $7 Billion for Firm's Biggest Bets [Read Full Article →](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-16/sequoia-s-new-leaders-raise-about-7-billion-for-firm-s-biggest-bets) economictimes.com #### Sequoia's new leaders raise about $7 billion for firm's biggest bets [Read Full Article →](https://m.economictimes.com/tech/funding/sequoias-new-leaders-raise-about-7-billion-for-firms-biggest-bets/articleshow/130324695.cms) thenextweb.com #### Sequoia raises $7 billion for its biggest-ever late-stage fund - AI Product.title: Why AI Fails, EV Startup Raises Cash and Escaping the Illusion of Excellence: IndustryWeek's Weekly Review - IndustryWeek
AI Product.titleZh: Why AI Fails, EV Startup Raises Cash and Escaping the Illusion of Excellence: IndustryWeek's Weekly Review - IndustryWeek
AI Product.content: [The Economy](/the-economy) # Why AI Fails, EV Startup Raises Cash and Escaping the Illusion of Excellence: IndustryWeek's Weekly Review Also, does lean or Six Sigma reign supreme? And how to improve employee engagement with financial education. April 17, 2026 2 min read Welcome to IndustryWeek's Weekly Review, where we showcase the most consumed content over the past seven days, based on our IndustryWeek manufacturing community's viewing habits. Artificial intelligence attracts significant attention in this edition, as does an automotive startup, continuous improvement and more. Enjoy. The Top 10 are: [EV Truck Startup Slate Raises $650M, Promises Deliveries This Year:](https://www.industryweek.com/leadership/companies-executives/news/55370732/ev-truck-startup-slate-raises-650m-promises-deliveries-this-year) The venture’s leaders say they have more than 160,000 reservations for their customizable vehicles. Next up: Taking preorders starting in June. [Trump's New Budget Eliminates MEPs; Eight States Without Contracts:](https://www.industryweek.com/leadership/article/55369519/trumps-new-budget-eliminates-meps-eight-states-without-contracts) Latest claim from Trump: DEI has ruined the program. [The Big Productivity Gains Will Come from Cross-Functional AI:](https://www.industryweek.com/technology-and-iiot/digital-tools/article/55363960/the-big-productivity-gains-will-come-from-cross-functional-ai) The interaction of generative AI, agentic AI and machine learning across different areas of an organization holds the greatest promise for optimizing talent. [AI Doesn't Fail Because of Technology. It Fails Because of Misplaced Friction:](https://www.industryweek.com/members/article/55369212/ai-doesnt-fail-because-of-technology-it-fails-because-of-misplaced-friction) Friction that encourages discourse, process understanding and problem-solving is essential and improves outcomes. [The Progress Paradox: Why Do We Feel Stuck When Everything Is Green?](https://www.industryweek.com/operations/continuous-improvement/podcast/55365800/the-progress-paradox-why-do-we-feel-stuck-when-everything-is-green-podcast) Dive into the topic of suboptimization, or the “illusion of excellence,” which occurs when individual departments focus on improving their own metrics at the expense of the overall enterprise. Learn tactics to drive enterprise-wide performance. [Another Chip Shortage Is on the Horizon:](https://www.industryweek.com/supply-chain/planning-forecasting/article/55369017/another-chip-shortage-is-on-the-horizon) With huge demand for high-bandwith memory, some semiconductor experts are predicting "RAMageddon." [Lean or Six Sigma: the Progress Paradox:](https://www.industryweek.com/operations/continuous-improvement/podcast/55368936/lean-or-six-sigma-the-progress-paradox) If you're talking continuous improvement and want to spark passionate debate, try this question: Which is better—lean or Six Sigma? Our podcast hosts tackle this turbulent topic. [Get Your AI Terminology Straight: A Manufacturing Leader's Guide:](https://www.industryweek.com/technology-and-iiot/emerging-technologies/article/55370572/get-your-ai-terminology-straight-a-manufacturing-leaders-guide) If you don't know your LLMs from your MLs and agents, you're far from alone. [Business Literacy Creates a Culture of Trust Among Our Employees:](https://www.industryweek.com/talent/engagement/article/55370224/business-literacy-creates-a-culture-of-trust-why-dont-more-manufacturers-teach-it-to-their-employees) Educating our manufacturing workforce on financials has increased engagement and saved money. [Fluke Puts Large Language Models to the Test:](https://www.industryweek.com/technology-and-iiot/article/55364958/fluke-reliability-puts-large-language-models-to-the-test) Fluke’s latest CMMS update includes AI-based tools meant to make life easier for maintenance technicians. Sign up for our eNewsletters Get the latest news and updates ## Related [### Making Edge AI Useful for Manufacturing]...

AI Product.title: Q2 2026 PitchBook Analyst Note: Agentic AI: The Evolution to Autonomous Systems: Part II - PitchBook
AI Product.titleZh: Q2 2026 PitchBook Analyst Note: Agentic AI: The Evolution to Autonomous Systems: Part II - PitchBook
AI Product.content: Q2 2026 # PitchBook Analyst Note: Agentic AI: The Evolution to Autonomous Systems: Part II ### **Agentic AI’s next chapter: Trust, integration, and the race for workflow ownership** Agentic AI has entered a deployment phase, but what does that actually look like in practice? In Part II of our Agentic AI series, we go beyond the data with exclusive interviews with founders and executives across 13 agentic AI startups, spanning cybersecurity, legal tech, enterprise content, customer support, pathology, financial services, outbound sales, autonomous robotics, and more. The picture that emerges is consistent across verticals: Adoption is concentrating in structured, high-volume workflows where outcomes are measurable and errors are reversible. Model capability is no longer the primary bottleneck: governance, integration depth, and organizational readiness are determining who deploys successfully and who stalls. Trust is emerging as an engineering challenge as much as a cultural one. Across industries, respondents independently converge on the same requirements for expanding autonomy: explainable decision paths, audit trails, confidence thresholds, and critically, the ability to undo mistakes. Durable value is shifting away from models and toward systems, with proprietary context, workflow ownership, and platform-level control becoming the new sources of defensibility. **Table of contents** GPT-Rosalind – named after esteemed UK chemist and X-ray crystallography pioneer Rosalind Franklin, whose research helped reveal the structure of DNA – is designed to help researchers with the early stages of drug discovery by making tasks such as evidence synthesis, hypothesis generation, and experimental planning quicker and more efficient. The new platform has been announced only a couple of days after Amazon unveiled its own AI-powered drug discovery platform – [Amazon Bio Discovery](https://pharmaphorum.com/news/amazon-launches-its-ai-drug-discovery-platform) (ABD) – that will compete with rival systems from the likes of [NVIDIA](https://pharmaphorum.com/tag/nvidia), [Alphabet/Isomorphic Labs](https://pharmaphorum.com/tag/isomorphic-labs), and [Anthropic](https://pharmaphorum.com/tag/anthropic), all designed to reduce the time it takes for new programmes to go from target discovery to the clinic. In its announcement, OpenAI called GPT-Rosalind a "frontier reasoning model built to support research across biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine," adding that it is already working with Amgen, Moderna, the Allen Institute, Thermo Fisher Scientific, and others to apply it to research and discovery workflows. "The life sciences field demands precision at every step. The questions are highly complex, the data are highly unique, and the stakes are incredibly high," said Sean Bruich, head of AI and data at Amgen. "Our unique collaboration with OpenAI enables us to apply their most advanced capabilities and tools in new and innovative ways with the potential to accelerate how we deliver medicines to patients." Open Source AI.content: [Skip to content](#main-content) [LDS Let's Data Science LEARN • BUILD • STAY AHEAD](/) [Sign in](/login?redirect=%2Fnews%2Fopenai-launches-gpt-rosalind-to-accelerate-drug-discovery-58ed5bf4) FOUNDING MEMBER SALE $3.99/mo 1,500+ Questions · 15 Domains · Unlimited Audio 492 spots left [Claim Your Spot](/pricing) Models & Research gpt rosalind life sciences openai drug discovery # OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind To Accelerate Drug Discovery | 7.8 Relevance Score OpenAI has released **GPT-Rosalind**, a purpose-built frontier reasoning model for life sciences research, optimized for chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics. The model is available as a research preview in ChatGPT, Codex, and via the API to qualified users through OpenAI's trusted access program, and it ships with a freely accessible Life Sciences research plugin that connects models to more than **50** scientific tools and data sources. OpenAI is already piloting the model with partners including **Amgen**, **Moderna**, the **Allen Institute**, and **Thermo Fisher Scientific**. GPT-Rosalind aims to speed early-stage discovery tasks such as evidence synthesis, hypothesis generation, and experimental planning, while OpenAI emphasizes human-in-the-loop validation and enterprise-grade security controls for regulated environments. ### What happened **OpenAI** is introducing **`GPT-Rosalind`**, a frontier reasoning model purpose-built for life sciences research and drug discovery, with a focus on chemistry, protein engineering, and genomics. The model is available as a research preview in **ChatGPT**, **Codex**, and the API for qualified customers through OpenAI's trusted access program. OpenAI also released a freely accessible Life Sciences research plugin that links models to over **50** scientific tools and data sources. Pilot partners include **Amgen**, **Moderna**, the **Allen Institute**, and **Thermo Fisher Scientific**. ### Technical details GPT-Rosalind is positioned as the first in a new life sciences model series optimized for multi-step scientific workflows rather than generic language tasks. OpenAI highlights improved tool use and deeper domain understanding as the core upgrades. Benchmarks cited by OpenAI and press coverage include leading performance on BixBench and mixed wins on LABBench2, where GPT-Rosalind outperformed GPT-5.4 on several tasks. The release emphasizes capabilities for: * • evidence synthesis across literature and databases, * • biological hypothesis generation and prioritization, * • experimental planning and protocol reasoning, * • integration with lab software, databases, and analysis tools via the Codex plugin. OpenAI also calls out enterprise-grade security controls and access management tailored to regulated environments, and it intends access through a trusted program rather than open, unrestricted release. ### Context and significance This is a strategic pivot from general-purpose foundation models toward domain-specialized reasoning systems that act as decision-support layers for expert workflows. The life sciences vertical is high value, with long time-to-market and high failure rates, making early-stage improvements economically impactful. Partnerships with large biopharma and infrastructure companies give OpenAI real-world validation pathways and data connectors that matter for adoption. The model follows an industry trend where ML teams and vendors ship verticalized reasoning models, and it signals OpenAI doubling down on enterprise and regulated sectors where compliance, auditability, and data governance are required. ### Practical implications for practitioners GPT-Rosalind is not a drop-in replacement for lab expertise, OpenAI and reporters stress. Practitioners should expect: * • faster literature triage and hypothesis scaffolding that can reduce cycle time in early discovery, * • opportunities to prototype end-to-end workflows by combining GPT-Rosalind with existing LIMS, ELN, and analysis stacks via the Codex plugin, *...

Open Source AI.title: OpenAI Has a New AI Model Built for Biology and Science - CNET
Open Source AI.titleZh: OpenAI Has a New AI Model Built for Biology and Science - CNET
Open Source AI.content: * [ai atlas](/ai-atlas/) * [Tech](/tech/) * Services & Software * [AI](/tech/services-and-software/ai/) # OpenAI Has a New AI Model Built for Biology and Science GPT-Rosalind is intended to help scientists streamline their research and drug discovery. Giselle Castro-Sloboda Fitness and Nutrition Writer I'm a Fitness & Nutrition writer for CNET who enjoys reviewing the latest fitness gadgets, testing out activewear and sneakers, as well as debunking wellness/fitness myths. In my free time I enjoy cooking, going for a scenic run, hitting the weight room, or watching a documentary. I am a former personal trainer and still enjoy learning and brushing up on my training knowledge from time to time. I've had my wellness and lifestyle content published in various online publications such as: Women's Health, Shape, Healthline, Popsugar and more. Expertise Fitness and Wellness [See full bio](/profiles/gcastro05/) Giselle Castro-Sloboda 2 min read OpenAI's latest AI model is built to do far more than offer cooking advice or create a spreadsheet. GPT-Rosalind, the company's first model specifically built for life science, is meant to help scientists with drug discovery, biology and translational medicine. The model is named after [Rosalind Franklin](https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-023-01313-5 "(opens in a new window)"), whose research revealed the structure of DNA and formed the foundations for modern molecular biology. Scientific research relies heavily on data, and GPT-Rosalind is designed to help sort through it, while also helping reduce the time it takes to develop and get new drugs approved and out on the market. (Disclosure: Ziff Davis, CNET's parent company, in 2025 filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging it infringed Ziff Davis copyrights in training and operating its AI systems.) It can take 10 to 15 years for a new drug to be developed and approved in the US, [OpenAI said in a blog post Thursday](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-rosalind/ "(opens in a new window)"). GPT-Rosalind is intended to improve the selection of research targets and create stronger hypotheses for higher-quality experiments. The model has been tested on topics such as its understanding of organic chemistry, proteins and genetics. Researchers can use it to find relevant scientific literature for their work or design experiments. This isn't the first time an AI model has been developed with medical advancements in mind. [Google DeepMind](https://www.cnet.com/tech/services-and-software/death-to-gmail-google-deepmind-ceo-wants-ai-to-solve-this-one-annoying-problem/) has developed many AI models for scientific research, such as AlphaFold, which earned its creators a share of the [2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry](https://www.cnet.com/tech/services-and-software/google-deepmind-scientists-win-nobel-prize-for-alphafold-ai-project/). "For me, the best use case for AI was to improve human health and accelerate scientific discovery," Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis said in a [recent interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=C0gErQtnNFE "(opens in a new window)"). Anthropic introduced [Claude for Life Sciences](https://www.anthropic.com/news/healthcare-life-sciences#link=%7B%22role%22:%22standard%22,%22href%22:%22https://www.anthropic.com/news/healthcare-life-sciences%22,%22target%22:%22%22,%22absolute%22:%22%22,%22linkText%22:%22Claude%20for%20Life%20Sciences%22%7D "(opens in a new window)") in January with the same purpose. Some scientists have expressed concerns in the past about how quickly AI has infiltrated the science space and have [warned of vulnerabilities, potential misuse](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-025-63913-1 "(opens in a new window)") and issues with data representation. OpenAI said GPT-Rosalind has safeguards to protect it from misuse -- like the creation of a biological weapon -- and has teamed up with various biotechnology, pharmaceutical and life sciences technology organizations to support research and scientific discov...

Open Source AI.title: Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma - VentureBeat
Open Source AI.titleZh: Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma - VentureBeat
Open Source AI.content: # Anthropic just launched Claude Design, an AI tool that turns prompts into prototypes and challenges Figma [Michael Nuñez](/author/michael_nunez) [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/) today launched [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs), a new product from its Anthropic Labs division that allows users to create polished visual work — designs, interactive prototypes, slide decks, one-pagers, and marketing collateral — through conversational prompts and fine-grained editing controls. The release, available immediately in research preview to [all paid Claude subscribers](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11049762-choosing-a-claude-plan), is the company's most aggressive expansion beyond its core language model business and into the application layer that has historically belonged to companies like [Figma](https://www.figma.com/), [Adobe](https://www.adobe.com/), and [Canva](https://www.canva.com/). [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs) is powered by Claude Opus 4.7, Anthropic's most capable generally available vision model, which the company also released today. Anthropic says it is rolling access out gradually throughout the day to Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers. The simultaneous launches mark a watershed for Anthropic, whose ambitions now visibly extend from foundation model provider to full-stack product company — one that wants to own the arc from a rough idea to a shipped product. The timing is also significant: Anthropic hit roughly [$20 billion in annualized revenue](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/anthropic-tops-30-billion-run-221045473.html) in early March 2026, according to Bloomberg, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025 — and surpassed $30 billion by early April 2026. The company is in early talks with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley about a potential IPO that could come as early as October 2026. ## **How Claude Design turns a text prompt into a working prototype** The product follows a workflow that Anthropic has designed to feel like a natural creative conversation. Users describe what they need, and Claude generates a first version. From there, refinement happens through a combination of channels: chat-based conversation, inline comments on specific elements, direct text editing, and custom adjustment sliders that Claude itself generates to let users tweak spacing, color, and layout in real time. During onboarding, Claude reads a team's codebase and design files and builds a design system — colors, typography, and components — that it automatically applies to every subsequent project. Teams can refine the system over time and maintain more than one. The import surface is broad: users can start from a text prompt, upload images and documents in various formats, or point Claude at their codebase. A web capture tool grabs elements directly from a live website so prototypes look like the real product. What distinguishes [Claude Design](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-design-anthropic-labs) from the wave of AI design experiments that have proliferated in the past year is the handoff mechanism. When a design is ready to build, Claude packages everything into a handoff bundle that can be passed to Claude Code with a single instruction. That creates a closed loop — exploration to prototype to production code — all within Anthropic's ecosystem. The export options acknowledge that not everyone's next step is Claude Code: users can also share designs as an internal URL within their organization, save as a folder, or export to Canva, PDF, PPTX, or standalone HTML files. Anthropic points to [Brilliant](https://brilliant.org/), the education technology company known for intricate interactive lessons, as an early proof point. The company's senior product designer reported that the most complex pages required 20 or more prompts to recreate in competing tools but needed only 2 in Claude Design. The Brilliant tea...
